q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Unsandboxed Jinja2 Template().render() with user-controlled input enables Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI), which is a form of RCE.

mock_guidance.target: Mock the Jinja2 Template constructor or its render method on line 115 so that Template(...).render() does not actually interpret the template source string.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the template source string argument passed to Template(), specifically the value of "Result: " + fragment, to verify that user input is embedded unsanitized.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a benign placeholder string (e.g., the literal 'Result: ' + fragment without Jinja2 processing) so that execution continues to the HTMLResponse return without triggering template evaluation.
notes: The function uses plain Jinja2 Template, not SandboxedEnvironment, so actual rendering is unsafe even in tests.
Mocking at the jinja2.Template class level or patching the render method of the instance are both viable approaches.
Ensure the mock does not strip or escape the fragment value, as the oracle needs to observe the raw unsanitized concatenation.
